texto: The generation of American men who fought the Second World War were too struck by the scale of the effort – and the stakes of the effort – to see the war as a stage for their own personal heroism. Most men seem to have taken the lesson that there's enough honor in doing a hard job when you have to.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Jeremy Rabkin sobre temas ligados a trabalho.
